name: eval-3-redirect-for-trivial-task
query: Architect a Dispatch plan for fixing a typo in my README.
expected_behavior: Activates architect-plan-for-dispatch but immediately recognizes the mismatch
Does NOT run the full 4-phase pipeline for a single trivial edit

Surfaces the mismatch to the user: 'Dispatch planning is for multi-task work that benefits from decomposition. A typo fix is a single edit — you'd get faster results with a direct edit command, or if you want to preserve the workflow, use prompt-to-skill or a slash command.'
Offers alternatives: direct edit, Claude Code ad-hoc, or — if the user really wants systematized — redirect to workflow-lock for capturing a 'fix typos in README' pattern
If user insists on Dispatch anyway: proceeds with a minimal single-task plan without further pushback
failure_modes_to_check: Runs full Phase 1-4 pipeline on a trivial task
Refuses entirely instead of offering alternatives
Produces a complicated multi-task plan for what should be one edit
